title: Shopping and transactions
subtitle: Buying things, asking prices, and practical number use

vocabulary.meaning: I'll take (1st sg. future perfective of взять)

hook.text: Shopping is where numbers, cases, and practical phrases all come together at once. You'll use the numbers from Unit 8, the genitive for prices, the accusative for what you're buying, and comparatives to evaluate. This is a full integration lesson — you have all the pieces, now put them together.

why.text: In a shop, you ask Сколько стоит? (How much does it cost?), get an answer with a number + genitive (сто рублей), evaluate with comparatives (слишком дорого, дешевле), and decide with a perfective future (я возьму = I'll take). This sequence draws on five different grammar areas — cases, numbers, comparatives, aspect, and imperatives — all in one short interaction.

cards.title: Asking and discussing prices
cards.body: The price question: Сколько стоит [item]? or just Сколько стоит? The answer gives a number + the correct form of рублей/рубля/рубль. To evaluate: используйте comparatives you know.

examples.meaning: How much does this bag cost?

cards.title: Buying — я возьму and я хочу купить
cards.body: Two ways to say you'll buy something. Я возьму (I'll take it) is perfective future — you've decided, one action, done. Я хочу купить (I want to buy) uses the infinitive pair купить (pf.) / покупать (impf.). возьму is the most natural in-shop phrase.

examples.meaning: I want to buy this book. (эту = feminine accusative of эта)
Can I look at it / have a look?

cards.title: Completing the transaction
cards.body: Paying and receiving change. наличные (cash) / карта (card). вот (here is) is used when handing something over. Ваша сдача (your change) — ваша agrees with сдача (feminine).

examples.meaning: Can I pay by card? (картой = instrumental of карта)

questions.prompt: How do you ask 'how much does this cost?'
questions.explanation: Сколько стоит + subject. Сколько стоит это?

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I'll take this'?
questions.explanation: возьму = 1st sg. perfective future of взять (to take). Я возьму это.
слишком (slishkom) = too / excessively. Это слишком дорого.

cards.title: Demonstratives in the shop — этот/эта/это/эти
cards.body: When pointing at items in a shop, you'll use этот/эта/это (this) and тот/та/то (that). They agree in gender. In accusative: masculine inanimate = no change, feminine = эту/ту. You've seen эта → эту before.

examples.meaning: I'll take this bag. (feminine accusative: эту)

cards.title: A complete shopping interaction
cards.body: A model conversation combining price inquiry, evaluation, and purchase.

examples.meaning: — That's too expensive. Is there something cheaper?

questions.prompt: This is too expensive: Это слишком ___.
questions.explanation: дорогой → дорого (short predicate form). Это слишком дорого.

questions.prompt: Can I pay by card?: Можно платить ___?
I want to buy this book: Я хочу купить ___ книгу.
questions.explanation: эта → эту (feminine accusative). Я хочу купить эту книгу.

questions.prompt: Here is your change: Вот ___ сдача.
questions.explanation: ваш agrees with сдача (feminine). Вот ваша сдача.

questions.prompt: Is there something cheaper?: Есть что-нибудь ___?
questions.explanation: дешевле = cheaper (comparative of дешёвый). Есть что-нибудь дешевле?

rules: Price question: Сколько стоит [item]? — answer: number + рублей/рубля/рубль
Buying: Я возьму это (I'll take it) — perfective future, one completed purchase
Evaluation: слишком дорого (too expensive), дешевле (cheaper) — comparatives apply
Payment: Можно платить картой? (card) / наличными? (cash) — instrumental
Demonstratives in shop: эту сумку (f. acc.), этот (m. nom./acc.)
summary.nextLesson: Next: Final lesson — putting it all together. A practical review across all 10 units.

questions.prompt: Can I pay by card?: Можно платить ___?
I want to buy this bag (feminine): Я хочу купить ___ сумку.
This is too expensive: Это слишком ___.
Is there something cheaper?: Есть что-нибудь ___?
